name: Issue quotes with HTTP / REST API and Airtable for your team

parameters.text: =✅ Issue quotes with HTTP / REST API and Airtable for your team — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf04077-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Gather API Numbers

### Gather API Numbers

GET https://api.example.com/v1/documents — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Blend Sources · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Gather from Airtable

Searches Airtable's 'Documents' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Blend Sources · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Blend Sources

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Roll Up KPIs

### Roll Up KPIs

Group-by + aggregate (like a pivot table) — turns raw rows into KPI numbers.

Next: Compose HTML Report

### Compose HTML Report

Custom JavaScript: Build an HTML digest table from the aggregated rows

Next: Send Report via Outlook

### Send Report via Outlook

Sends an email via Microsoft Outlook — recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Ports: ①→ Tag Pipeline Metadata · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Sort Documents Newest First

### Sort Documents Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Outlook

### Escalate Failure to Outlook

Sends an email via Microsoft Outlook — recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

# Issue quotes with HTTP / REST API and Airtable for your team

**Pattern:** Document Generation · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Issue quotes with HTTP / REST API and Airtable for your team. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual ocr & extraction is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Gather API Numbers → Blend Sources → Roll Up KPIs → Compose HTML Report → Send Report via Outlook → Tag Pipeline Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to document generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating ocr & extraction in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Airtable PAT; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

KPIs are rolled up with Summarize (exact group-by + aggregate) before any AI touches the data — asking an LLM to 'figure out the numbers' from raw records is slower, costlier, and less reliable than doing exact arithmetic in n8n and only asking the model to narrate numbers that are already correct.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Add a second data source and watch it blend into the same report via Merge.
2. Toggle the AI executive-summary step on and off and compare the report's usefulness.
3. Change the KPI grouping field and see how the pivot reshapes.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your ocr & extraction process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
